title: 10 best herbal teas for hyperthyroidism

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha tea helps to balance cortisol levels, reducing the negative effects of stress on thyroid function and alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

Antioxidant properties: Ashwagandha's antioxidants combat free radicals that can damage thyroid tissue and disrupt its functioning, which is particularly important in hyperthyroidism where inflammation is often present.

Thyroid-regulating properties: Ashwagandha tea has been shown to regulate thyroid hormone production and secretion, helping to reduce the excessive production of thyroid hormones that occurs in hyperthyroidism.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera, also known as ashwagandha, teas helps with hyperthyroidism because it has been shown to regulate thyroid function by reducing the production of thyroid hormones. The adaptogenic properties of ashwagandha help to balance the body's response to stress, which can contribute to an overactive thyroid gland. By calming the thyroid gland and reducing inflammation, ashwagandha tea may help alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism, such as anxiety, tremors, and weight loss. This natural approach may offer a complementary therapy for those seeking to manage their condition.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: Withanolides, particularly withanone and withaferin A, have been shown to possess anti-thyroid properties, helping to reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

Saponins: Saponins present in Withania somnifera tea may help regulate thyroid function and reduce oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism, contributing to a decrease in thyroid hormone production.

Alkaloids (Withanine and Withanolide D): Alkaloids in Withania somnifera tea have been found to have antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate the oxidative stress caused by excessive thyroid hormone production, thereby alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Withania somnifera are commonly used to make teas for hyperthyroidism due to their adaptogenic properties, which help balance the body's hormonal levels.
Leaves: The leaves of Withania somnifera are used to make teas for hyperthyroidism as they contain antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds that help regulate the body's response to stress.
Barks: The barks of Withania somnifera are used to make teas for hyperthyroidism due to their potential to help reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate hyperthyroid symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2-3 grams of dried withania somnifera root per 8 oz of water.
Steep the root in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids from the liquid.
Add honey or other sweeteners to taste and adjust the flavor as desired.
Allow the tea to cool completely before serving or storing it in airtight containers.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate thyroid issues by calming the immune system and reducing inflammatory responses.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, scavenging free radicals and protecting against damage to thyroid tissue, which can occur due to hyperthyroidism-induced oxidative stress.

Glycyrrhizic acid: Inhibits the production of a hormone called 5-lipoxygenase, which is involved in inflammation and immune responses, potentially reducing thyroid hormone production and alleviating hyperthyroid symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with hyperthyroidism because it contains compounds that help regulate cortisol levels and stabilize thyroid hormone production. The flavonoids and glycosides present in licorice root tea have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, promoting a balanced thyroid function. Additionally, licorice root tea may help reduce the conversion of T4 to T3, a process that contributes to hyperthyroidism, making it a potentially beneficial herbal remedy for individuals with this condition.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Glycosides (Glycyrrhizin): These compounds may help alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms by reducing cortisol levels, which can help mitigate the negative effects of excess thyroid hormones on the body.

Flavonoids (Isoliquiritigenin and Liquiritigenin): These phenolic compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help counteract the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism.

Triterpenoid Saponins (Glycyrrhetic Acid): These compounds may help stabilize and regulate cortisol levels, leading to a decrease in thyroid hormone levels and a reduction in symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and cortisol-balancing properties to help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Leaves: Used for their adaptogenic properties to help regulate the body's response to thyroid hormone imbalances.
Barks: Used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted source and wash them thoroughly with water.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the dried roots in 1 liter of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release flavors.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large container to remove solids.
Discard the solids and transfer the liquid to a clean container for storage and refrigeration at 4 degrees celsius.
Serve the tea chilled or at room temperature within 24 hours of preparation in small quantities of 100 milliliters.

remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and triterpenoids, can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, which is often exacerbated in cases of hyperthyroidism.

Liver support: Dandelion tea has been shown to stimulate liver function, which may help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism by facilitating the removal of excess thyroid hormones from the bloodstream.

Thyroid hormone regulation: The flavonoids and triterpenoids present in dandelion tea may have a regulating effect on thyroid hormone production, helping to normalize thyroid function and reduce symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism, such as anxiety, tremors, and rapid heartbeat.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with hyperthyroidism because of its natural ability to support thyroid function and promote detoxification. The roots and leaves of the plant contain compounds that stimulate the liver and kidneys to remove excess thyroid hormones from the body, thereby reducing hyperthyroid symptoms. Additionally, dandelion tea has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate thyroid-related inflammation and promote a balanced hormonal environment, making it a potential complementary remedy for managing hyperthyroidism.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Taraxacum officinale tea have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the severity of hyperthyroidism symptoms by minimizing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with the condition.

Phenolic acids (e.g., chlorogenic acid and quercetin): Phenolic acids in Taraxacum officinale tea have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-thyroid properties, which can help protect against thyroid damage and regulate thyroid hormone production.

Flavonoids (e.g., kaempferol and isorhapontigenin): Flavonoids in Taraxacum officinale tea have been reported to exhibit anti-thyroid and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism by reducing thyroid hormone production and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their antithyroid and diuretic properties to help manage hyperthyroid symptoms.
Roots: They are used for their antithyroid and anti-inflammatory properties to help regulate thyroid function and alleviate hyperthyroid symptoms.
Flowers: They are used for their antithyroid and antioxidant properties to help balance thyroid hormones and reduce oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Gather taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in a quantity of about 2 teaspoons per cup of boiling water.
Steep the taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Add honey or other sweeteners to taste and stir well to dissolve the sweetness evenly.
Serve the taraxacum officinale tea immediately and consume within 30 minutes to 1 hour.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Maca tea helps to balance the body's response to stress, reducing symptoms of anxiety and fatigue associated with hyperthyroidism.
Antioxidant properties: Maca tea's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage thyroid tissue, potentially slowing or reversing thyroid damage caused by hyperthyroidism.
Thyroid-regulating properties: Maca tea may help regulate thyroid hormone production, reducing symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lepidium meyenii, also known as maca, teas helps with hyperthyroidism because it contains unique compounds that help regulate thyroid function. The tea's active ingredients, such as glucosinolates, are believed to stimulate the thyroid gland and promote a balance between thyroid hormones. Maca tea has been traditionally used in Peruvian medicine to support thyroid health and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism, including weight loss, anxiety, and irregular heartbeat. Its potential to regulate thyroid function makes it a promising natural remedy for managing hyperthyroidism.
remedy_constituents: Glucosinolates: These sulfur-containing compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism by modulating the immune system and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which help mitigate the damage caused by excess thyroid hormones on the thyroid gland and other organs, thereby alleviating hyperthyroidism symptoms.

Iodine and its derivatives: Iodine and its derivatives, such as iodine-containing glucosinolates, help regulate thyroid function and reduce the production of thyroid hormones, thus addressing the underlying cause of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used to make teas for hyperthyroidism due to their high content of goitrogen compounds that help regulate thyroid function.
Roots: The roots are used to make teas for hyperthyroidism due to their high concentration of glucosinolates, which have been shown to have a regulatory effect on the thyroid gland.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60g of lepidium meyenii roots and clean them thoroughly with water.
Chop the roots into small pieces and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind 3-5g of dried lepidium meyenii root into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powder in 200ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool before serving.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate thyroid inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to hyperthyroidism.

Thyroid-regulating effects: The flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba tea may help regulate thyroid function by inhibiting the conversion of thyroxine (T4) to triiodothyronine (T3), thus reducing excessive thyroid activity.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which is often a hallmark of hyperthyroidism, and alleviate related symptoms such as heat intolerance and joint pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with hyperthyroidism because of its ability to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba may help to counteract the excessive thyroid hormone production associated with hyperthyroidism. Additionally, its vasodilatory properties can help to lower blood pressure, a common symptom of hyperthyroidism. By improving circulation and reducing oxidative stress, Ginkgo biloba teas may help to alleviate some of the symptoms of hyperthyroidism and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): These flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism.
Bilobalide: This terpenoid compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

Ginkgolides (Ginkgolide A and B): These terpenoid compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, potentially reducing thyroid hormone production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary part used for Ginkgo biloba teas, as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help mitigate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism, such as anxiety and palpitations.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used in teas, particularly for their antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress often linked to hyperthyroidism.
Buds: Ginkgo biloba buds contain flavonoids and terpenoids similar to those found in leaves, which may aid in reducing symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as rapid heartbeat and tremors.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh ginkgo biloba leaves or 1-2 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted source.
Rinse the ginkgo biloba leaves gently with cool water to remove any dirt or debris instantly.
Combine 1 cup of boiling water with 1-2 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in a teapot for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the ginkgo biloba leaves immediately.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature before serving and consuming the tea slowly.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Herbal teas like Ashwagandha, Ginseng, and Rhodiola rosea contain adaptogenic compounds that help the body adapt to stress and normalize thyroid function by reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, teas helps with hyperthyroidism because it contains high levels of L-theanine, an amino acid that promotes relaxation and reduces stress levels. The tea's unique blend of catechins, specifically EGCG, may also help slow down the production of thyroid hormones, thereby alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism. remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used part of Camellia sinensis to make teas, such as green tea, which may help alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

Buds: Young tea buds are also used to make teas, like white tea, which may help in reducing the symptoms of hyperthyroidism due to their high antioxidant content and ability to regulate thyroid function.

Stems: Some teas, like yellow tea, use the stems of Camellia sinensis, which may also contribute to reducing hyperthyroidism symptoms due to their ability to regulate thyroid hormone production and balance body heat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 green tea leaves and 10-20 flowers from camellia sinensis plants in the morning.
Clean the harvested leaves and flowers by gently rinsing with cold water for 2 minutes.
Heat 8 ounces of water to 160-170 degrees fahrenheit in a teapot for 5 minutes.
Steep 1 teaspoon of green tea leaves and 2 flowers in the hot water for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the tea and serve it immediately in cups for consumption.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for teas due to their high content of rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and thyroid hormone production.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers when the plant is in peak growth.
Rinse the harvested leaves and flowers with cold running water for 30 seconds to remove dirt.
Steam the leaves and flowers for 5 minutes to release their essential oils and flavors.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried mentha x piperita in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and serve it immediately after preparation to preserve its flavor and aroma.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make teas for hyperthyroidism due to their potential anti-thyroid properties.
Barks: Used to make teas for hyperthyroidism due to their possible ability to reduce thyroid activity.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of freshly dried ulmus rubra bark in the early morning or late evening.
Chop the dried bark into small pieces using a sharp knife or a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the chopped bark in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy the medicinal properties within 10-15 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and fatty acids, may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism symptoms.

5-alpha-reductase inhibition: Saw palmetto's active compound, beta-sitosterol, has been shown to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can exacerbate thyroid issues and hyperthyroidism symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Saw palmetto tea's antioxidant compounds may help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism development.

Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, contributing to a decrease in thyroid hormone levels and a reduction in hyperthyroid symptoms. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds may help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism by modulating the activity of thyroid hormones and reducing oxidative stress in the body.

Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid): Phenolic acids in Serenoa repens may possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could help mitigate the inflammatory response associated with hyperthyroidism and protect the body from oxidative damage.

remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried serenoa repens root powder for a standard serving size.

Steep the serenoa repens root powder in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine mesh sieve into a cup to remove the powder residue.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Guggul teas have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism-related symptoms such as fatigue and weight loss.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Commiphora mukul, also known as guggul, teas helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain compounds that may help regulate thyroid hormone production. remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried commiphora mukul bark and 15-30 grams of dried roots from a trusted source.
Grind the harvested commiphora mukul bark and roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine 2-3 teaspoons of the ground commiphora mukul powder with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the tea reaches the desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine mesh strainer and discard the solids immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with hyperthyroidism because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The main compound, curcumin, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, alleviating symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, tremors, and weight loss. Cytokine modulation: Turmeric tea's bioactive compounds have been shown to modulate cytokine production, which can help regulate the immune system's response and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as fatigue and joint pain.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of turmeric tea can help protect against oxidative damage caused by excess thyroid hormone production in hyperthyroidism, which can lead to tissue damage and organ dysfunction.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of the turmeric plant contains a high concentration of bioactive compounds like curcumin, which helps to regulate thyroid hormone levels and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 teaspoons of dried turmeric root in 2 cups of water for 10 minutes to create a flavorful decoction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl to remove solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey and stir well to sweeten the tea according to individual taste preferences.
Let the tea cool to room temperature for 30 minutes before refrigerating it in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.
Reheat the cooled tea by simmering it gently over low heat for 5 minutes or until warm and soothing to drink.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers for maximum potency and flavor.
Combine one teaspoon of dried or two teaspoons of fresh foeniculum vulgare with boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavonoids and volatile oils to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea into a cup using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve, discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Drink one cup of foeniculum vulgare tea three times daily for 6-8 weeks, with meals, to alleviate hyperthyroid symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers from a reputable source.
Boil one liter of distilled water in a large saucepan over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Add the dried crataegus monogyna mixture to the boiling water and steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea into a large cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it and consuming up to three cups daily.

By soothing the thyroid gland, sassafras tea may help hyperthyroidism patients achieve a state of equilibrium and reduce symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Sassafras tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as joint pain and swelling.

Adaptogenic Properties: Sassafras tea has adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress, including hormonal imbalances caused by an overactive thyroid gland. This can help regulate thyroid function and reduce symptoms like anxiety and fatigue.

Thyroid-Supportive Compounds: Sassafras tea contains compounds such as safrole and lignans that have been shown to support thyroid health by increasing the production of thyroid hormones and improving thyroid function, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in beta-sitosterol, a compound that helps reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
Bark: Contains safrole, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that can help soothe the thyroid gland and reduce inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sassafras albidum bark in early spring or fall when it's most potent and aromatic.
Chop 1 tablespoon of dried sassafras albidum bark or 2 tablespoons of fresh bark into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped sassafras albidum with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot or infuser, steeping for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids and discard the solids, retaining 8 ounces of tea liquid.
Drink the tea three times a day, sipping slowly over 20-30 minutes, ideally one hour after breakfast, lunch, and dinner.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with hyperthyroidism because it contains compounds that have a natural anti-inflammatory effect, which can help reduce the symptoms of an overactive thyroid gland. The antioxidants and volatile oils present in ginger also help to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress associated with hyperthyroidism. Additionally, ginger's ability to stimulate digestion and improve nutrient absorption may help alleviate symptoms such as diarrhea and weight loss common among individuals with hyperthyroidism. Overall, ginger teas can be a natural and effective way to manage the symptoms of this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and body, which is often exacerbated in hyperthyroidism.

Antioxidant property: The high antioxidant content in ginger tea helps neutralize free radicals that can damage thyroid cells and tissues, promoting a healthy thyroid function and reducing oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism.

Digestive aid property: Ginger tea's digestive benefits help alleviate nausea, bloating, and other gastrointestinal symptoms commonly experienced by individuals with hyperthyroidism, allowing for better nutrient absorption and overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of ginger contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is the thickened stem of ginger that stores nutrients, making it rich in bioactive compounds that can help regulate thyroid hormones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots of zingiber officinale for optimal antioxidant properties and flavor.
Wash the harvested ginger roots thoroughly under cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Slice the washed ginger roots into thin pieces using a sharp knife or grater to release its active compounds.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of sliced ginger in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to infuse its bioactive molecules.
Strain the tea solution through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and store refrigerated for up to 24 hours.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hyperthyroidism that are treated with herbal teas are adrenal fatigue, thyroiditis, and Hashimoto's disease. Herbs like ashwagandha, guggulu, and licorice root are commonly used in teas to help alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, weight loss, and rapid heart rate. These herbs work by supporting the adrenal glands and regulating cortisol levels, helping to reduce stress and promote overall thyroid health.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hyperthyroidism are natural remedies that help alleviate symptoms associated with an overactive thyroid gland. These teas work by reducing inflammation, balancing hormones, and promoting relaxation. Examples include peppermint, chamomile, and ashwagandha teas, which have been shown to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and regulate thyroid function. By incorporating these teas into their daily routine, individuals with hyperthyroidism can experience improved overall well-being, increased energy levels, and a reduced reliance on medication.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with hyperthyroidism is a blend of Ashwagandha, Peppermint, and Licorice root teas. Ashwagandha helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate hyperthyroid symptoms. Peppermint tea aids in digestion and calms the mind, while Licorice root tea helps to balance thyroid function and reduces inflammation. Drinking these teas together in moderation can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as palpitations, tremors, and weight loss. It's essential to consult a healthcare professional before making any changes to your treatment plan.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by International Journal of Biological Macromolecules, researchers investigated the impact of polyherbal extract (POH) of four medicinal plants to treat hyperthyroidism. The study involved a 18-day in vivo experiment where POH was administered to Wistar albino rats with L-thyroxine-induced hyperthyroidism. The results showed that POH therapy significantly increased TSH, T3, and T4 levels, restored thyroid function to normal, and reduced the increase of liver enzymes caused by hyperthyroidism, indicating that POH may offer a protective and effective alternative treatment to synthetic thyroid medications for hyperthyroidism.
